key_insight: A hybrid IFENN framework can model both initiation and propagation of phase-field fracture across new geometries by coupling FEM mechanics with pre-trained neural surrogates for the phase-field equation.
engineering_relevance: This offers a faster, generalizable surrogate workflow for fracture simulation that reduces offline training cost while preserving physics-based accuracy in solid mechanics applications.
method: Couples a standard FEM equilibrium solver with a neural network surrogate for the coupled phase-field diffusion equation.
Uses DeepOKAN for fracture initiation and a CNN for crack propagation.
Trains once on a benchmark geometry with physics-informed sampling from the fracture process zone and uses artificial boundary conditions for far-field extrapolation.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports successful testing across both the training geometry and unseen geometries, with training based on only a few increments and limited strategically sampled Gauss points.
watch_item: The far-field correction via artificial boundary conditions may be sensitive to domain changes and should be validated carefully when extrapolating to very different crack configurations.
paper_title: A hybrid IFENN solver for generalizable modeling of phase-field fracture initiation and propagation
